nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission Only. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. This section is intended for CESS undergraduate majors who are designing, conducting, working with a faculty member on a research project, or finalizing an honors thesis. Students are expected to schedule time at the beginning of the semester to meet with their honors thesis advisor or CESS liaison with the Patrick Leahy Honors College (PLHC) to organize their work for the semester.

nodes.title: Soc Process & Institutionl Chg
nodes.description: Critical analysis of theory and research related to justice, caring, and change in education and other social institutions. Focus: ideology, diversity, and management of knowledge.
nodes.other_requirements: Doctoral Students Only or Instructor Permission

nodes.title: Teach Asst: Foundations of Ed
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Soc, Hst & Phil Found of Educ
nodes.description: Critical examination of central educational/social issues and values with special emphasis on the struggle for justice and equality. Themes include schooling and social class, race, and gender; the purposes of education; and the responsibilities of teachers. Prerequisite: Completion of a 2000 level course.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Unplugged
nodes.description: See Schedule of Course for specific titles.

nodes.title: School and Society
nodes.description: As a foundational course in the College of Education and Social Services, this course investigates the interplay between schooling and society. We examine how children and youth are situated within the context of systems, including their families, communities, and the larger society, that influence their development and learning. We will explore the sociological purposes of schools, their historical development in the US, and the resulting inequitable impact of school organization and policy.

nodes.title: Intro to Research Methods
nodes.description: Seminars and research projects. Methods of historical, descriptive, experimental, quasi-experimental, field studies, and survey research.

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Permission of the Coordinator of Professional Laboratory Experiences.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission of the Coordinator of Professional Laboratory Experiences.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission; Students must be volunteering, tutoring, or working at a PreK-12 school setting.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis. Thesis topic must be approved by a faculty committee.
nodes.other_requirements: Thesis topic must be approved by a faculty committee. Instructor Permission Only

nodes.title: Race and Racism in the U.S.
nodes.description: Students will investigate the multi-faceted concepts of identity, racism, and the dynamics of power, privilege, and oppression in the United States.
